summary: Implemented a design-only DinoBrain Observatory graph redesign based on the provided dinosaur-shaped graph reference. Kept OS/MCP/SQLite/hook/data logic unchanged; updated only the Observatory visual layout/rendering CSS and browser-side canvas layout. The graph now uses deterministic dinosaur pose targets so live nodes and edges read as tail, body/core, neck/head, and leg clusters. Verified desktop and mobile rendering and saved a Product Design QA report with final result passed.
decisions: Design-only scope: no OS, MCP, SQLite, hook, installer, or data-model behavior changed.
Promoted the graph panel to a full-width first-screen visual surface and moved detail panels below it.
Used browser-side pose targets rather than altering source graph data, preserving live node and edge semantics.
Reduced active-task label density so the silhouette stays readable.
next_steps: Review the local Observatory at http://127.0.0.1:3951/ and decide whether the next iteration should add a graph-only presentation mode for a more literal full-viewport dinosaur view.
Commit/push the design branch if the visual direction is accepted.
